  CONFIGURE  ·  Say what you want — then launch
═════════════════════════════════════════════════

A Cherny loop iterates an agent toward a goal until a gate proves it met — or a
budget stops it. **Say what you want in your own words.** When you describe ad
hoc work, the gate planner chooses a script gate, an agent-review gate, or a
hybrid of both based on what would actually prove the work is done.

Goal

_(not yet chosen — say what you want, or `configure goal=…`)_

Artifact under test:  (none — the maker works from the repo)

The gate

**planner** — no gate is locked in yet. Send a goal in your own words and the
planner will choose whether this should be verified by a script, a prompt, or
both.

Budgets

Iteration cap:  8
Cost cap:       (disabled)

Actions:

  ✗ launch — say what you want first
  abort                               abandon the loop
  look
